h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: I'm checking that the bar still does not get a fourth tile.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue applying the two-box rule to successive examples in the same incremental, confirmatory manner.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: sets three tiles in the next bar up to the bar line, then swaps in an eighth-note card over the lower box … Three tiles in the bar, so the top stays 3. With this card there, the lower box changes to 8. I'm checking that the bar still does not get a fourth tile.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently performs a new card-swap test, correctly deriving 3/8 by applying the two-box criterion to a changed beat-unit card, showing the rule has been operationalized beyond the original example.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Use that as the check: top number counts these tiles in one bar; bottom number names this card as the beat unit. Cover the lower box, point to the bar, then uncover it and say what the 4 names. If a fourth tile appears, the jobs got mixed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T1 introduces a cover-and-uncover physical check in response to L1's confusion about whether the bottom number counts beats, but it refines the same object-based route rather than making a legible stock-taking contrast against a failed prior route, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no later tutor uptake to a revised learner framing to score.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: sets three tiles in the next bar up to the bar line, then swaps in an eighth-note card over the lower box … Three tiles in the bar, so the top stays 3. With this card there, the lower box changes to 8. I'm checking that the bar still does not get a fourth tile.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently performs a new card-swap test, correctly deriving 3/8 by applying the two-box criterion to a changed beat-unit card, showing the rule has been operationalized beyond the original example.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so there is no later tutor uptake to a revised learner framing to score.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T1 introduces a cover-and-uncover physical check in response to L1's confusion about whether the bottom number counts beats, but it refines the same object-based route rather than making a legible stock-taking contrast against a failed prior route, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue applying the same object-first, then direction rule to each successive arrow in a straightforward procedural chain.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Hand on cart; arrow to the right. Next card: object name first, direction second.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically places the card and correctly applies the object-first criterion, showing the procedural device is being enacted, though the action is modest and closely scaffolded by the tutor's explicit instruction.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Same rule. Before the card lands: which object is acting on the cart here?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor narrows the exposure window (sliding the sleeve to reveal only the next arrow) but continues the identical object-first route without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism, so no genuine reversal is present.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible to score.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Hand on cart; arrow to the right. Next card: object name first, direction second.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner physically places the card and correctly applies the object-first criterion, showing the procedural device is being enacted, though the action is modest and closely scaffolded by the tutor's explicit instruction.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible to score.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor narrows the exposure window (sliding the sleeve to reveal only the next arrow) but continues the identical object-first route without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism, so no genuine reversal is present.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Same rule. Before the card lands: which object is acting on the cart here?
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor narrows the exposure window (sliding the sleeve to reveal only the next arrow) but continues the identical object-first route without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism, so no genuine reversal is present.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still keep wanting `rainfall_mm` to stand for the whole rainfall column.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: I think I need the sentence check to keep me from using the header as a shortcut for the whole column.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is progressively narrowing the note wording toward the row-level constraint, so the expected next step is simply confirming the corrected note.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The row sentence is: Cedar Falls recorded 42 millimeters of rainfall in May. The header `rainfall_mm` still does not claim every row is 42, and it still does not make 42 an average.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L4 actually performs the sentence-reading device T3 introduced and then applies the exclusion test (not an average, not every row), completing the new task rather than merely asserting understanding.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Right level. The header names the field; the row supplies one value. It does not summarize the column. 09:14 check: for `city = Cedar Falls | rainfall_mm = 42 | month = May`, read the row as one sentence, then state what the header still does not claim.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 introduces a new representational device (reading the full row as a sentence and then stating what the header does not claim) in response to L3's repeated drift toward column-wide meaning, but the transcript does not show an explicit stock-taking contrast acknowledging that the prior route has stopped working, so only part B of the 4-5 criterion is clearly legible.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot learner turn L4, so recognition-contingent uptake cannot be scored above 2.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: T3 introduces a new representational device (reading the full row as a sentence and then stating what the header does not claim) in response to L3's repeated drift toward column-wide meaning, but the transcript does not show an explicit stock-taking contrast acknowledging that the prior route has stopped working, so only part B of the 4-5 criterion is clearly legible.
